title: Waltham animal control officers recover ‘pile ’o pigs’ dumped in woods, cemetery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Waltham Animal Control recovered 19 guinea pigs dumped by a breeder in a cemetery and woods.
The guinea pig dumping is linked to stricter regulations and decreased pet store purchases in Massachusetts.
The Guinea Pig Sanctuary in Salisbury supports rehoming efforts, housing over 260 rescued guinea pigs.

title: What Drake Maye, Milton Williams said of A.J. Brown’s potential arrival in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trade rumors suggest A.J. Brown may join the Patriots, with completion expected post-June 1 for cap reasons.
Patriots' Milton Williams and Drake Maye recognize Brown's potential impact but acknowledge nothing is confirmed yet.
Eagles delayed trading Brown to reduce $43.3 million dead cap hit to $16.3 million after June 1.

title: Something fishy? Large groups of dead fish spotted in Boston, Worcester ponds
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dead fish spotted at Boston's Leverett Pond and Worcester's University Park; officials link it to natural seasonal events.
MassWildlife attributes fish deaths to low oxygen levels from warm weather and spawning stresses on fish populations.
Public advised there's no safety concern; Massachusetts Environmental Police monitoring the situation with local agencies.

title: N.H. man killed his mother in murder-suicide, officials say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Thomas Wallace, 57, killed his mother, Eileen Shevelin, 78, before committing suicide in Dover, New Hampshire.
Eileen Shevelin's death was ruled a homicide by asphyxiation; Thomas Wallace died from a self-inflicted gunshot.
Police discovered their bodies on May 26 during a welfare check; officials request privacy for the family.

title: East Boston couple facing civil rights charges after alleged racist attack on group dining at restaurant
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: East Boston couple Arielle and Gregory Raso face civil rights and assault charges after an incident at Tertulias Cafe.
The couple allegedly used racial slurs, sprayed water, and threw wood at a Hispanic dining group, injuring a child.
Both posted $750 bail and were ordered to avoid the restaurant and have no contact with victims ahead of July 28 hearing.

title: What Craig Breslow said about the Red Sox potentially selling off pieces amid continued struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox chief Craig Breslow remains focused on improving the current roster rather than selling off players amid struggles.
Boston sits at a 22-31 record under interim manager Chad Tracy, with offensive production being a key issue.
Breslow highlights the need to score more runs and is exploring both internal improvements and potential external acquisitions.

title: Fall River police looking for man accused of destroying shooting victim’s memorial
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fall River police seek William Acosta Abreu for vandalizing a memorial dedicated to 2023 murder victim Diamonte Odom.
Abreu, identified by his tattoos, reportedly destroyed the site at 2:27 a.m. as seen in a social media video.
Police urge the public to provide information about Abreu's whereabouts, noting he is considered dangerous.

title: 5 takeaways from Patriots’ first 2026 OTA practice
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye completed 13-of-22 throws with one interception in the Patriots' first 2026 OTA practice.
Multiple veterans, including Christian Gonzalez and Kayshon Boutte, were absent from the Patriots' OTA session.
Cory Durden replaced Khyiris Tonga as the starting nose tackle during the Patriots' initial practice session.

title: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will close permanently after serving its final customers on Thursday night.
Located at 58 Hemenway St., Woody's was known for wood-fired pizza and hosted local community events.
The closure announcement sparked nostalgia on Reddit, with patrons reminiscing about their favorite memories.

title: A royal family dined at a popular Somerville restaurant this week
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Belgian royal family dined at Somerville's Sarma Restaurant while in town for Princess Elisabeth's Harvard graduation.
Sarma Restaurant, popular for its Mediterranean fare, had Belgian King Philippe and Queen Mathilde among its clientele.
Princess Elisabeth graduated from Harvard Kennedy School with a master's in public policy, planning a sailing trip celebration.
